title: CommandButton
text: CommandButton The CommandButton is a logical widget that enables a user to initiate an action by clicking the button. For more information, see Specifying a Widget for a Field or Entity . Table 1. Widget Overview Logical widget name: CommandButton Maps to physical widget: ucmdbutton Default definition: CommandButton=ucmdbutton(tooltip=on;font=buttonfont;actOnEachClick=on) Supported triggers and properties: See ucmdbutton . Use for: Static or control fields (as defined by the Is Static and Is Control properties) of type String. Supports Accessibility? Yes, for testing purposes and screen readers only. For more information, see Accessibility . Description The specified action is defined in the Detail trigger of the button field. Because this field is not used for data input, it is usually defined as a static or control field. Tip: Uniface provides templates for command buttons that perform common actions such as retrieving and storing data, adding and removing entity occurrences, and more. By default, the CommandButton follows the Windows look and feel. If the Representation property is set to Uniface or Header , the appearance is different. For more information, see UnifaceButton and HeaderButton . Related Links Defining Command Buttons Command buttons enable the user to perform specific actions. They should only be used in form components, not in reports. KeyPadEnterAccess Specifies whether the keypad Enter key is routed through the keyboard translation table for command buttons.
